title: Consolidate _parse_sse_body into shared test helper
analysis: _parse_sse_body is defined identically in tests/test_web_e2e.py (line 66) and tests/test_sse.py (line 39). Both parse SSE wire-format text into a list of event dicts with id/event/data keys. The only difference is the type annotation on the local variable: test_web_e2e.py uses `events: list[dict] = []` while test_sse.py uses `events = []`. The fully-typed version from test_web_e2e.py should be canonical. tests/conftest.py already has a shared plain helper function (extract_json_blocks at line 127) following the same pattern — module-level function, no fixture decorator, with docstring. The function is used 4 times in test_web_e2e.py and 7 times in test_sse.py, always as a bare call. Since _parse_sse_body is a plain function (not a pytest fixture), both test files need explicit imports. The conftest.py import path is `from tests.conftest import _parse_sse_body`. The change is purely structural — no behavioral change to any test.

steps.description: Write a focused test that imports _parse_sse_body from tests.conftest and verifies it parses SSE wire-format text correctly: multi-event bodies, trailing event without blank line terminator, and id/event/data field extraction. This test will fail because _parse_sse_body does not yet exist in conftest.py.

Add _parse_sse_body to tests/conftest.py after the existing extract_json_blocks helper (after line 139). Use the fully-typed signature from test_web_e2e.py (`events: list[dict] = []`). The import test from step-1 should now pass.
Run pytest tests/test_sse.py tests/test_web_e2e.py as a baseline to confirm all existing tests pass before removing the duplicate definitions.
Remove the _parse_sse_body definition from tests/test_sse.py (lines 39-59) and add `from tests.conftest import _parse_sse_body` to the imports at the top of the file.
Run pytest tests/test_sse.py to verify all 10 SSE tests still pass after replacing the local definition with the conftest import.
Remove the _parse_sse_body definition and its section comment from tests/test_web_e2e.py (lines 61-86) and add `from tests.conftest import _parse_sse_body` to the imports at the top of the file.
Run pytest tests/test_web_e2e.py tests/test_sse.py to verify all tests in both files pass after both duplicates are removed and both files import from conftest.

design_decisions.decision: Use explicit import (`from tests.conftest import _parse_sse_body`) rather than relying on pytest conftest auto-discovery
design_decisions.rationale: pytest auto-discovery only works for fixtures (decorated with @pytest.fixture). _parse_sse_body is a plain helper function, so it must be explicitly imported. This is consistent with how extract_json_blocks (another plain helper in conftest.py) would be used.

design_decisions.decision: Use the fully-typed signature `events: list[dict] = []` from test_web_e2e.py as the canonical version
design_decisions.rationale: The task description specifies using the fully-typed signature. The test_sse.py version uses bare `events = []` which loses the type annotation. Both are functionally identical.

conftest.py organizes code as: imports, internal helpers, fixtures, public helper functions. extract_json_blocks is already at the end (line 127). Adding _parse_sse_body after it maintains this grouping.

design_decisions.decision: No separate helpers module — add directly to conftest.py
design_decisions.rationale: The task explicitly specifies conftest.py as the destination. The existing pattern (extract_json_blocks) already lives there. A separate helpers.py would add unnecessary indirection for two helper functions.

reuse.how: Copy the fully-typed version verbatim as the canonical shared helper

reuse.what: Existing plain helper function pattern in conftest.py (extract_json_blocks, line 127)
reuse.how: Follow the same style: module-level function, no fixture decorator, with docstring, placed at file bottom
